meta.title: Services
meta.description: Technical services and support from QS

index.intro: In modern manufacturing, CNC technology is the foundation that determines the machining productivity of the entire factory. Yet long-term operation inevitably leads to equipment wear: faults arise, performance degrades, and mechanical precision is lost — directly affecting product quality and production schedules.

solution.heading: End-to-end optimisation
solution.lead: To solve this problem at the root, QS Technology delivers a comprehensive CNC machine reconditioning and upgrade solution. With a team of engineers who understand the technology and carry hands-on experience across a wide range of machines, QS Technology confidently diagnoses the exact cause, fully resolves the fault, and restores your machine to peak operating performance.
pillars: Diagnose and fully resolve electrical-system issues.
Upgrade the controller to the modern QS Astro platform.
Restore and fine-tune the precision of the mechanical assembly.

detailPage.includesHeading: What's included and not included in the retrofit package

retrofit.name: Retrofit
retrofit.lede: Replace legacy Fanuc/Siemens/Mitsubishi controllers with QS F-series or Astro. Keep the mechanics, motors, and electrical cabinet — only the electronics and HMI change. Save 70–80% versus buying a new machine, with a typical ROI under 18 months.

process.title: On-site survey
process.desc: An FAE visits your workshop to measure the mechanics, check motors and the electrical cabinet, and list parts to keep or replace.

process.title: Proposal & quote
process.desc: A detailed BOM-based quote, proposed wiring diagram, rollout schedule, and expected machine downtime.

process.title: Preparation & programming
process.desc: Prepare the controller, write custom firmware for your machine, and build the cabinet at the QS workshop — your machine keeps running.

process.title: On-site installation
process.desc: Remove the old controller, install QS, wire it up, test the axes, and calibrate. 2 engineers stay on-site until it's done.

process.title: Training & acceptance
process.desc: Train the operators, run 12 real parts, and accept against the QS checklist. Handover with a 24-month warranty.

includesIntro: QS has standardised its retrofit process across 240+ projects. You get a complete package — no worrying about missing parts or finding extra labour. The items below are standard; some machines may need additions.
For specialised machines (5-axis, bending, purpose-built), QS offers a dedicated package — please book a survey for an accurate quote.

includes.name: QS F-series or Astro controller
includes.note: Chosen by axis count & precision needs

packages.name: [ Pkg · Basic ]
packages.title: Old 3-axis milling
packages.priceNote: Per machine · F54 controller · incl. VAT

packages.name: [ Pkg · Pro · most popular ]
packages.title: 4–6-axis milling
packages.priceNote: Per machine · F86 / Astro 6AH · incl. VAT

faqs.a: Yes, if the mechanics (rails, ball-screws) still hold acceptable tolerances. QS measures during the survey and advises — sometimes ball-screws need replacing first for the retrofit to be worthwhile.

faqs.q: Can I keep my old G-code programs?
faqs.a: Yes. QS provides post-processors for most legacy formats (Fanuc, Siemens 840D) with automatic conversion. Complex macros may need minor tweaks — engineers help free of charge for 30 days.
On average 3 business days. If the machine is a line bottleneck, QS can work evening and weekend shifts to cut it to 36–48h, with a 15% surcharge.

faqs.q: Can I still use Mastercam as before after the retrofit?
faqs.a: Yes. QS provides a Mastercam post-processor (2023+) free of charge. The CAM programmer's workflow barely changes — only the .pst file is swapped.

cta.title: Book an on-site survey — free, no obligation
cta.desc: A QS FAE will visit your workshop within 5 business days. You'll receive a detailed quote and a concrete downtime plan within 3 days afterward.
